title: The 2025 Movie Star Rankings: 35 Over 35. Plus: Nicolas Cage!
format: Sean and Amanda rebuild the 35-over-35 movie-star ranking, from Julia Roberts to Leonardo DiCaprio, after a review of Another Simple Favor. Then Sean talks to Nicolas Cage about The Surfer.

segments.heading: The state of the industry
segments.note: the Trump movie tariff and a windowing gripe

films.title: Highest 2 Lowest
films.note: Spike Lee's fifth with Denzel, an Apple/A24 remake of Kurosawa; opens in theaters August 22, on Apple TV+ two weeks later, which is what set Sean off.

films.title: High and Low
films.note: The Kurosawa original being remade.

films.title: Inside Man
films.note: Denzel's last crime drama with Spike Lee; $185M.

films.title: American Gangster
films.note: $267M, cited to argue Denzel still sells theatrical.

films.title: The Little Things
films.note: Day-and-date during the pandemic and still made $30M.

films.title: Parasite
films.note: Part of the global-cinema wave the tariff talk ignores.

films.title: The Instigators
films.note: The Apple dump that made Amanda nervous about Highest 2 Lowest.

films.title: The Gorge
films.note: Another Apple straight-to-streaming release Sean is annoyed by.

films.title: Fountain of Youth
films.note: Same complaint.

films.title: Another Simple Favor
films.note: The peg for the whole episode: two 35-over-35 stars, neither of whom made the list. Sean thought it was bad, Amanda enjoyed the first 20 minutes of pure Kendrick-Lively rapport before it went broad.

films.title: A Simple Favor
films.note: The original, which made $97M in theaters and had a toxic curiosity this sequel trades for Dirty Rotten Scoundrels farce.

films.title: Dirty Rotten Scoundrels
films.note: The con-artist farce the sequel actually resembles.

films.title: It Ends with Us
films.note: Part of Blake Lively's recent run and her image problem; Sean finds her better as a villain than as someone sympathetic.

films.title: The Town
films.note: Where Lively is a real asshole, i.e. her best mode.

films.title: Bridesmaids
films.note: Feig's real-comedy peak that Amanda wishes he'd return to.

films.title: The Heat
films.note: Another Feig theatrical comedy.

films.title: The Accountant
films.note: Amanda's example of Anna Kendrick's 'why are you here' frequency.

films.title: Deadpool & Wolverine
films.note: Lively's cameo, part of her recent stretch.

films.title: IF
films.note: Lively's voice work; also Krasinski's directorial effort, mentioned repeatedly later.

segments.heading: Toughest cuts and the Materialist test
segments.note: who missed, and the three stars riding on one movie

films.title: Thor: Love and Thunder
films.note: Christian Bale (Sean's toughest cut), good in a bad movie.

films.title: The Pale Blue Eye
films.note: Bale, on Netflix.

films.title: Amsterdam
films.note: Bale; a disaster that made no money, orders of magnitude worse than a Hemsworth flop.

films.title: Extraction
films.note: Chris Hemsworth (Amanda's toughest cut): huge on Netflix, but never a draw outside a franchise.

films.title: Furiosa: A Mad Max Saga
films.note: Hemsworth is great in it, but it didn't perform.

films.title: Bad Times at the El Royale
films.note: The last original theatrical movie Hemsworth made.

films.title: Crime 101
films.note: Hemsworth, Halle Berry, Barry Keoghan, Mark Ruffalo; a Michael Mann-ish crime movie, his next test.

films.title: Nope
films.note: Daniel Kaluuya's last leading role; where has he been?

films.title: Judas and the Black Messiah
films.note: Kaluuya, arguably his last lead before Nope.

films.title: The Lost City
films.note: Sandra Bullock's last leading role; $200M, then she stopped taking parts.

films.title: Bullet Train
films.note: Bullock's cameo since.

films.title: The Fabelmans
films.note: Seth Rogen as a critical character, not the lead.

films.title: An American Pickle
films.note: Rogen's last real star vehicle.

films.title: Glass Onion: A Knives Out Mystery
films.note: Janelle Monae looked like a movie star, then stopped making movies.

films.title: IF
films.note: John Krasinski wrote, directed, and played the creepy father; voice/support doesn't count.

films.title: Ballerina
films.note: Ana de Armas: not out yet, so not eligible.

films.title: No Time to Die
films.note: de Armas, one great scene.

films.title: Wake Up Dead Man: A Knives Out Mystery
films.note: Daniel Craig, still making them (for Netflix).

films.title: Queer
films.note: Craig's snub, which Sean reads as a stature demerit.

films.title: Monkey Man
films.note: Dev Patel: a swing Sean liked, but not there yet.

films.title: Song Sung Blue
films.note: Hugh Jackman, a sneaky awards contender at year's end.

films.title: Road House
films.note: Jake Gyllenhaal, fallen back into streaming movies and TV.

films.title: Blink Twice
films.note: Zoe Kravitz, cut.

films.title: Madame Web
films.note: Dakota Johnson, coming off a tough stretch; a Materialist-test star.

films.title: Materialists
films.note: Out in a month; Dakota Johnson, Chris Evans, and Pedro Pascal are all off the list pending it.

films.title: Eddington
films.note: Pedro Pascal's Ari Aster movie; part of the loaded slate that still isn't enough for Sean yet.

films.title: Gladiator II
films.note: Pascal, okay in it.

films.title: Wonder Woman 1984
films.note: Pascal, fine.

films.title: The Adjustment Bureau
films.note: Pascal as the maitre d'.

films.title: The Great Wall
films.note: Pascal opposite Matt Damon.

films.title: Leave the World Behind
films.note: No. 35 Julia Roberts: one of Netflix's five most-watched movies ever.

films.title: After the Hunt
films.note: No. 35 Julia Roberts: the Luca Guadagnino campus drama keeping her eligible, maybe an Oscar play.

films.title: Jurassic World Rebirth
films.note: No. 34 Mahershala Ali: the quippy Harrison Ford part.

films.title: Spider-Man: Beyond the Spider-Verse
films.note: No. 34 Mahershala Ali: the Prowler, arguably the key voice.

films.title: The Odyssey
films.note: No. 33 Anne Hathaway: the most anticipated movie in the world, and how she got onto the list.

films.title: Mother Mary
films.note: No. 33 Anne Hathaway: David Lowery's A24 film, playing a pop star.

films.title: The Idea of You
films.note: No. 33 Anne Hathaway: Michael Showalter directed her last one.

films.title: Flowervale Street
films.note: No. 33 Anne Hathaway: David Robert Mitchell, pushed 16 months, apparently has dinosaurs.

films.title: Maestro
films.note: No. 32 Bradley Cooper: a leading role, and a maniac's movie Sean loves.

films.title: Nightmare Alley
films.note: No. 32 Bradley Cooper: the other lead of the memorial 50-something slot.

films.title: Is This Thing On?
films.note: No. 32 Bradley Cooper: his next as director; whether he qualifies hinges on how big his role is.

films.title: A Quiet Place: Day One
films.note: No. 31 Lupita Nyong'o: doing a lot of the eligibility work.

films.title: 12 Years a Slave
films.note: No. 31 Lupita Nyong'o: the Oscar.

films.title: Top Gun: Maverick
films.note: No. 30 Glen Powell: the launch.

films.title: Hit Man
films.note: No. 30 Glen Powell.

films.title: Twisters
films.note: No. 30 Glen Powell: legitimate fame.

films.title: The Running Man
films.note: No. 30 Glen Powell: the fall tentpole, plus the secretive J.J. Abrams movie.

films.title: Everything Everywhere All at Once
films.note: No. 29 Michelle Yeoh: the Oscar win.

films.title: Wicked
films.note: No. 29 Michelle Yeoh: a big star even if Sean thinks the performance stinks.

films.title: Crazy Rich Asians
films.note: No. 29 Michelle Yeoh: her persistent recent run.

films.title: A Minecraft Movie
films.note: No. 28 Jason Momoa: chicken jockey; the thing that makes his case.

films.title: Aquaman
films.note: No. 28 Jason Momoa.

films.title: The Fall Guy
films.note: No. 28 Jason Momoa, amusingly, at the end.

films.title: In the Hand of Dante
films.note: No. 28 Jason Momoa: the Julian Schnabel movie.

films.title: Roofman
films.note: No. 27 Channing Tatum: Derek Cianfrance's return, based on the true story of a McDonald's robber hiding in a Toys R Us.

films.title: The Lost City
films.note: No. 27 Channing Tatum: part of his tight comeback stretch.

films.title: Magic Mike's Last Dance
films.note: No. 27 Channing Tatum.

films.title: G20
films.note: No. 26 Viola Davis: the one Sean promised to watch and didn't.

films.title: Air
films.note: No. 26 Viola Davis: they made this list right after it last time, which flattered her ranking.

films.title: The Hunger Games: The Ballad of Songbirds & Snakes
films.note: No. 26 Viola Davis: Amanda hated it; her sister Grace called it a classic.

films.title: Uncut Gems
films.note: No. 25 Adam Sandler: the high point before a semi-muted stretch.

films.title: Happy Gilmore 2
films.note: No. 25 Adam Sandler: dropping on Amanda's birthday.

films.title: Jay Kelly
films.note: No. 25 Adam Sandler: the Noah Baumbach movie, number-two role, lots of Oscar buzz.

films.title: Die My Love
films.note: No. 24 Jennifer Lawrence (a cheat, not 35 until August): the Lynne Ramsay film added to Cannes.

films.title: No Hard Feelings
films.note: No. 24 Jennifer Lawrence: quite good and very funny.

films.title: Causeway
films.note: No. 24 Jennifer Lawrence.

films.title: Oppenheimer
films.note: No. 23 Emily Blunt: the Oscar nomination that tacks onto her ranking.

films.title: The Fall Guy
films.note: No. 23 Emily Blunt: really good.

films.title: The Smashing Machine
films.note: No. 23 Emily Blunt: the A24 movie with The Rock, plus the Spielberg UFO movie, i.e. being knighted.

films.title: Here
films.note: No. 22 Tom Hanks: a performance Amanda loved.

films.title: The Phoenician Scheme
films.note: No. 22 Tom Hanks: the Wes Anderson ensemble; they're not talking about it.

films.title: Toy Story 5
films.note: No. 22 Tom Hanks.

films.title: Greyhound
films.note: No. 22 Tom Hanks: a sequel is coming; nobody can recall the first.

films.title: Tár
films.note: No. 21 Cate Blanchett: what they were coming off last time.

films.title: Black Bag
films.note: No. 21 Cate Blanchett: doing a lot of work here.

films.title: Borderlands
films.note: No. 21 Cate Blanchett: a fiasco, not her fault.

films.title: The Fantastic Four: First Steps
films.note: No. 20 Robert Downey Jr.: Doctor Doom, the reveal that popped.

films.title: Avengers: Doomsday
films.note: No. 20 Robert Downey Jr.: back to Marvel, to Sean's mild dismay.

films.title: Ballerina
films.note: No. 19 Keanu Reeves: brought in for reshoots to work the John Wick magic.

films.title: Good Fortune
films.note: No. 19 Keanu Reeves: an angel in the Aziz Ansari comedy.

films.title: Something's Gotta Give
films.note: No. 19 Keanu Reeves: yeoman's work as an unreasonably hot doctor.

films.title: The Old Guard 2
films.note: No. 18 Charlize Theron.

films.title: Fast X
films.note: No. 18 Charlize Theron: the thin recent stretch that has Amanda begging her to make more movies.

films.title: The Garfield Movie
films.note: No. 17 Chris Pratt: renowned for his work as Garfield.

films.title: The Electric State
films.note: No. 17 Chris Pratt: 'Jim State.'

films.title: Mercy
films.note: No. 17 Chris Pratt: a Minority Report-for-brain-injuries pitch, with Rebecca Ferguson; it looked entertaining.

films.title: Mickey 17
films.note: No. 16 Robert Pattinson: not a huge hit, but Sean thinks people are too hard on it.

films.title: The Batman
films.note: No. 16 Robert Pattinson: he's Batman, Batman 2 pending.

films.title: The Drama
films.note: No. 16 Robert Pattinson: the A24 drama with Zendaya.

films.title: Barbie
films.note: No. 15 Margot Robbie: the phenomenon, Amanda's No. 2 of its year.

films.title: Babylon
films.note: No. 15 Margot Robbie: where she went from 'are we sure she's good' to mattering.

films.title: Wuthering Heights
films.note: No. 15 Margot Robbie: the Emerald Fennell one Sean is concerned about.

films.title: A Big Bold Beautiful Journey
films.note: No. 15 Margot Robbie: September, opposite Colin Farrell.

films.title: Joker: Folie à Deux
films.note: No. 14 Joaquin Phoenix: the disaster in a tricky stretch.

films.title: Napoleon
films.note: No. 14 Joaquin Phoenix: didn't hit here, did well in Europe.

films.title: Eddington
films.note: No. 14 Joaquin Phoenix: the new Ari Aster.

films.title: A Minecraft Movie
films.note: No. 13 Jack Black: a huge movie star, Minecraft plus Mario plus Kung Fu Panda plus Jumanji.

films.title: The Super Mario Bros. Movie
films.note: No. 13 Jack Black.

films.title: Anaconda
films.note: No. 13 Jack Black: a tongue-in-cheek riff on the '90s original.

films.title: Deadpool & Wolverine
films.note: No. 12 Ryan Reynolds: we're living in his century.

films.title: Free Guy
films.note: No. 12 Ryan Reynolds.

films.title: The Adam Project
films.note: No. 12 Ryan Reynolds: the Spielberg-esque sci-fi mode Sean can't stand.

films.title: Project Hail Mary
films.note: No. 11 Ryan Gosling: the Vegas footage looked great.

films.title: Star Wars: Starfighter
films.note: No. 11 Ryan Gosling: directed by Shawn Levy, and 'Star Wars' is the premium brand name.

films.title: The Fall Guy
films.note: No. 11 Ryan Gosling: the more appealing of the two Ryans, per the hosts.

films.title: Air
films.note: No. 10 Matt Damon: 'pretty good,' but a movie about marketing.

films.title: The Odyssey
films.note: No. 10 Matt Damon: the vault-up, plus Oppenheimer.

films.title: The Rip
films.note: No. 10 Matt Damon: with Ben Affleck, whose mantle Matt is carrying.

films.title: The Smashing Machine
films.note: No. 9 Dwayne Johnson: 'is it The Rock's Oscar time?' after five stinkers in a row.

films.title: Moana 2
films.note: No. 9 Dwayne Johnson: amusing; a soundtrack Sean's warmed to.

films.title: Red One
films.note: No. 9 Dwayne Johnson: part of the cold streak.

films.title: Avatar: Fire and Ash
films.note: No. 8 Zoe Saldana: Neytiri, the biggest films of all time.

films.title: Emilia Pérez
films.note: No. 8 Zoe Saldana: what an Academy Award gets you, a big jump from 27.

films.title: Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 3
films.note: No. 8 Zoe Saldana.

films.title: Sinners
films.note: No. 7 Michael B. Jordan: he did it, just a movie star; a huge, cool movie heading past $300M.

films.title: Creed III
films.note: No. 7 Michael B. Jordan.

films.title: The Thomas Crown Affair
films.note: No. 7 Michael B. Jordan: his next, directing and starring, with Taylor Russell reportedly.

films.title: F1
films.note: No. 6 Brad Pitt: a huge amount riding on it.

films.title: Wolfs
films.note: No. 6 Brad Pitt: not great.

films.title: Once Upon a Time in Hollywood
films.note: No. 6 Brad Pitt: the David Fincher / Tarantino Cliff Booth movie is coming, for Netflix.

films.title: Jurassic World Rebirth
films.note: No. 5 Scarlett Johansson: so she's fine, whatever the scandals.

films.title: Asteroid City
films.note: No. 5 Scarlett Johansson: fantastic in it.

films.title: Black Widow
films.note: No. 5 Scarlett Johansson: the Disney pay dispute era she's climbed out of.

films.title: Poor Things
films.note: No. 4 Emma Stone: the second Oscar, one of four with Yorgos Lanthimos.

films.title: La La Land
films.note: No. 4 Emma Stone: 'the star of La La Land is niche,' Sean's rebuttal to himself.

films.title: Bugonia
films.note: No. 4 Emma Stone: her fourth with Lanthimos, out this fall.

films.title: Highest 2 Lowest
films.note: No. 3 Denzel Washington, the only septuagenarian on the list; the Spike Lee movie coming soon.

films.title: Gladiator II
films.note: No. 3 Denzel Washington: the best part, by a margin.

films.title: Malcolm X
films.note: No. 3 Denzel Washington: Ben Affleck called it the greatest screen performance of all time.

films.title: The Equalizer 3
films.note: No. 3 Denzel Washington: the best part of that too.

films.title: Mission: Impossible - The Final Reckoning
films.note: No. 2 Tom Cruise, down from No. 1; Sean sees it in a week, a huge moment in his life.

films.title: One Battle After Another
films.note: No. 1 Leonardo DiCaprio, up on vibes; the only person who could get PTA's movie made and released this way. A true box-office test.

films.title: The Surfer
films.note: Cage's new film with Lorcan Finnegan: an enigmatic, nonlinear story with a psychedelic streak. He talks about the prop rat, the punk-rock-Billy-Wilder 'eat it' scene, and choosing parts he feels he doesn't have to act.

films.title: The Swimmer
films.note: The strongest reference point; he heard Lancaster's voice in his head during one scene. Cheever's story is arguably more powerful than the movie.

films.title: Wake in Fright
films.note: New to him; the writer and director brought it up as an Ozploitation touchstone.

films.title: Walkabout
films.note: Nicolas Roeg; broke his heart as a child, an unrequited love story.

films.title: Picnic at Hanging Rock
films.note: Peter Weir; a bigger influence on him than the Ozploitation label.

films.title: The Last Wave
films.note: Peter Weir.

films.title: Face/Off
films.note: John Woo had him watch Bullet in the Head to show how far the performance could go.

films.title: Bullet in the Head
films.note: The Woo film whose operatic scale told Cage what Woo would let him do.

films.title: Hard Boiled
films.note: The Woo classic Cage already knew.

films.title: The Killer
films.note: The other Woo classic he knew going in.

films.title: Matchstick Men
films.note: Cage's pick for an underrated film of his; he chose it over Mystic River, and thinks it's one of Ridley Scott's best.

films.title: Mystic River
films.note: The Clint Eastwood role (Robbins's part) he passed on for Matchstick Men.

films.title: Vampire's Kiss
films.note: The self-referential lineage, taking on Dracula years later.

films.title: The Unbearable Weight of Massive Talent
films.note: Part of the recent run of parts in conversation with his own career.

films.title: Dream Scenario
films.note: Christopher Borgli saw Wild at Heart and knew that performance style would work here.

films.title: Wild at Heart
films.note: The style younger filmmakers keep reintroducing; The Surfer has some of it.

films.title: Vivarium
films.note: Cage saw Finnegan's film in the cinema and responded to it.

films.title: Sabrina
films.note: The Billy Wilder 'eat it' bit he riffed on with ketchup.

films.title: Like Father, Like Son
films.note: Cage's last-great pick: Kore-eda's swapped-at-birth drama, some of the best acting he's ever seen.

films.title: East of Eden
films.note: James Dean; why he got into cinema in the first place.

films.title: Enter the Dragon
films.note: Bruce Lee moving everywhere: one pole of compelling screen presence.

films.title: Once Upon a Time in the West
films.note: Charles Bronson doing nothing and riveting: the other pole. 'You just are that.'

Wake Up Dead Man: A Knives Out Mystery.title: Wake Up Dead Man: A Knives Out Mystery
